entry.title: Re: ModuleError: failed to load 'unit/unitTests.qunit.js'
entry.content: i am getting the same error can you help please

entry.title: Re: Launch SAP BW process chain from Fiori
entry.content:
You are right. There are many statements which cannot be used in RAP. You might try to call a wrapper function which starts this process in a background process. 

Also please format your code using the editor available.

entry.title: Re: Internal Order Budgeting
entry.content:
Ideally the technical clearing account is balance sheet account have you activated record account assignment for this GL it should not be possible, system allow CE category 90 or record account assignment for GL maintained in AO90, check with SAP they should be able to provide correction note.

Also check note 2274763

entry.title: Re: eDocument not not relevant"
entry.content:
The eDocument creation from billing documents is automatic and managed via a settings within FIBF transaction in the OP systems. So ideally they should appear instantly as the regular billing documents get posted and the accounting entries are released. Topic E28 from KBA 2530509 - Electronic Document Processing Framework Troubleshooting Guide - SAP for Me, should give you some idea.

The main problem may, eDocument appearing as 'Not Relevant', may occur due to certain missing customization, which country solution are you using? Every country solution will have certain shared customizations(from the eDocument Framework) and some related to the country solution itself. Here's an example of Greece eDocument solution Customizing | SAP Help Portal. Have you checked for the similar customization option for your country specific solution?

entry.title: Re: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Thanks for getting back to me, yes we are visiting old school UI technology, and I recall being trained on BSPs the back in 2004 (still got the SAP manuals) back when SDN was the place to go (guess most content was lost).

Yes just to clarify that these are legacy classic custom BSP apps (mixture of HMTL and ABAP), so yes definitely not SAPUI5, although as you correctly state they both show in SE80 as BSP apps.  The apps date from circa 2007.

They appear to still be functional in S/4HANA 2023 and Work Zone, despite the fact as you state this is considered old UI technology and no-one in their right mind today would consider using BSPs.

I assume the correct way forward is to refactor these to Fiori Freestyle using current Fiori design guidelines.  The fact this has not happened in the current ECC system prior to now is something of an oversight and before my time at this organisation, but I 'd personally be inclined to refactor as part of S/4HANA transformation activities (WRICEF/TOSL), and on a modern stack, ideally building on the BTP stack, rather than attempt to refactor to SAPUI5 on an ECC system where UI5 libraries, versions and theming are not current.  Deploying BTP to ECC stack is not currently an option either.  

I suppose the question is if these still run from FLP in S/4HANA private, like WDA or classic GUI reports and transaction also, will SAP at some point stop these legacy UIs from working.  I've also been searching for something that clearly expresses that this legacy UI technology is not supported in S/4HANA, but to no avail and my experience suggests that if technology is no longer supported, it broadly continues to function in S/4HANA. I guess given the level of organisations still running these older UI technologies on-prem or in private cloud, and not all legacy standard transactions have suitable Fiori alternatives, that would require an advance communication exercise that should be fairly easy to locate.

Thanks, Steve

entry.title: Re: How to customize tax codes and depreciation rules in...
entry.content:
Hello @AlbertFeng 

Below is a summarized Tax code and depreciation rules

Requirement

Recommended Approach

Special Depreciation Methods

Create custom depreciation keys using SSCUI (Manage Your Solution)

Set up additional depreciation areas (e.g., for tax vs IFRS reporting)

 

Rwandan Tax-Specific Adjustments

Use manual journal entries or custom fields to capture tax treatment.

 Use BAdIs or key user extensibility if automated tax calculation is needed.

 

Reporting & Compliance

Use standard reports (e.g., Asset History Sheet) or custom CDS views

Export to Excel or SAP Analytics Cloud for tax authority formats

 

System Limitations & Compliance

stay within Public Cloud restrictions (no backend ABAP mods, only approved logic)

This reflects SAP’s official extensibility strategy for Public Cloud. If Rwanda’s depreciation rules require functionality not available via SSCUIs or extensibility, you may need to:

  • Raise a feature request via SAP Influence Portal, or
  • Consider SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, which allows full customization

Let me know it helps

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: I can't find a Depreciation key for qatar region that calculated depreciation days based.
entry.content: I can't find a Depreciation key for the Qatar region that calculates depreciation days based on. Is there any way to do it?

entry.title: Optimizing Financial Services with AI: A Seamless Invoice Inquiry Resolution Journey
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of finance operations, managing supplier communications efficiently is crucial to maintaining trust and transparency. With the integration of AI-powered tools, finance teams are now better equipped to handle invoice inquiries with speed and precision. Here's how AI transforms a typical overdue invoice inquiry into a seamless, intelligent support experience.

🔑Key Capabilities

Here are the core capabilities enabled by Generative AI in SAP Enterprise Service Management:

Capability Description
Business Information ExtractionExtracts structured data (like Invoice ID, Order Number) from unstructured inputs such as email subject and body.
Document ExtractionParses structured data from PDF/image attachments (e.g., invoices, checks) using AI-trained schemas.
Document ClassificationAutomatically categorizes documents based on predefined classes (e.g., Invoice, PO, Receipt).
Auto-Executable ExtractionAutomatically extracts and populates fields in the business document tab without agent intervention.
Semantic Business Document TypesMaps extracted data to meaningful backend business entities (Invoice, Order, Request).
Integration with SAP S/4HANAEnables real-time lookup, validation, and posting of documents to SAP S/4HANA and other systems.
Prompt-Based AI GuidanceCustom scenario and schema prompts to enhance extraction precision.

✅Prerequisites

Before implementing these capabilities, ensure the following components and configurations are in place:

Technical Prerequisites

Requirement Description
SAP Service Cloud V2 / SAP Enterprise Service ManagementEnsure the latest release is in use.
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)For workflow, AI core models, and integration runtime.
SAP Integration Suite (CPI)To connect SAP Service Cloud V2 with SAP S/4HANA or external systems.

Functional Prerequisites

  • Enabled Case Management module.

  • Defined Case Types and Business Document Types.

  • Prepared Sample Documents (emails, invoices) for schema/scenario training.

🧠 Architecture Overview

 

📩Invoice Inquiry Email Initiation

It all begins with a supplier noticing an overdue invoice. Seeking clarification, the supplier sends an email to the finance team, including the invoice number and an attachment of the original invoice document. This initiates a service journey that blends human expertise with AI-driven support.

 

🚨Real-Time Access Notification to Open a Case

As soon as the email is received, the Finance Support Agent gets an automated alert indicating a new case assignment. This real-time notification ensures that no inquiry falls through the cracks and that agents can respond promptly, improving overall service responsiveness.

 

🗂️ Verifying and Categorizing the Case

The Finance Support Agent accesses the case, reviewing essential metadata such as:

  • Subject
  • Case Type
  • Supplier Name
  • Status
  • Priority

Thanks to intelligent workflows, even newly onboarded agents can consistently classify and prioritize cases, laying the groundwork for a standardized and scalable support process.

🧠 AI-Generated Case Summary

To reduce manual effort and ensure nothing is missed, an AI engine generates a concise summary of the case. This summary consolidates all relevant data points—invoice number, supplier, query type, and more—helping the agent quickly understand the context and make faster decisions.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

🔍AI-Driven Invoice Data Extraction

The agent leverages AI-based data extraction tools to identify and analyze the details within the attached invoice. These technologies reduce the need for manual data entry and ensure accuracy when verifying key information such as:

  • Invoice number
  • Amount
  • Due date
  • Payment status

 

✉️AI-Powered Email Drafting

Using an AI email assistant, the Finance Support Agent crafts a clear, professional response. The email outlines the blocked status, explains the missing items, and requests the delivery of the remaining widgets. It also confirms that payment will be processed upon receipt of the full order.

✅Resolution and Case Closure

Once the supplier receives the response and acknowledges the resolution path, the agent updates the case status to "Completed." The entire process—from inquiry to resolution—demonstrates the power of AI-enhanced workflows in accelerating and improving financial support services.

💡Key Takeaways

  • Real-time alerts drive faster case initiation.
  • AI-generated summaries speed up decision-making.
  • Data extraction tools ensure accurate invoice validation.
  • AI email drafting simplifies professional communication.
  • Standardized workflows ensure consistent case handling.

🚀The Future of Finance Support with SAP

This scenario showcases how SAP solutions integrated with AI capabilities can optimize finance support operations. By automating repetitive tasks, offering intelligent insights, and streamlining communications, finance teams can deliver exceptional supplier experiences—all while maintaining operational efficiency.

Settings for AI scenarios

Generative AI Scenarios --> Business Information Extraction

Step 1 – Define Input Sources
Identify if the case input will come from subject, description, or attachments. Choose based on where relevant information (e.g., invoice number) is typically found.

Step 2 – Configure a Business Information Extraction Scenario
If using subject/description, define a scenario and extraction elements (like Invoice ID). This guides the language model to extract info from free text.

 

Step 3 – Configure a Document Extraction Schema
If attachments are used, create a schema with header fields and descriptions. This enables structured extraction from documents like checks or invoices.

 

Step 4 – Create a Business Document Type
Define a document type with relevant columns (e.g., Invoice ID, Status). Columns pull data from extraction or backend API and allow manual entry if needed.

entry.title: I want to understand how cost travel from sales order, purchase order;
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to understand how the cost travels from the sales order to the purchase order. Are there any resources that you can share with me.

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Display Two Sales Org Addresses on MSDS
entry.content:
Hello

thanks for more infos..

Coming back to:

To display a Sales Org address, we rely on a custom table that maintains a 1:1 Country-to-Sales Org mapping. This has worked well so far, as each country typically has a centralized headquarters whose address can be displayed as the primary one.

However, Chile poses an exception. There is no centralized HQ; instead, smaller countries like Chile operate via distributed centers. Using the address of CL1 when the shipment originates from CL2 has already led to customer complaints due to inaccurate SDS address information.

i still have some doubt if i have understood your "landscape" correct.

My logic run like: you can define your sales organisations based on needs. You can define (if needed) two sales organisations located in Chile.

So my "simple" story would be: the real sales is done by sales org 1 or sales org 2. The SAP Standard chapter one story exactly support it like that.

Reading between the line: you have prepared a "custom driven country <> Address logic)

Which is any how customer driven.

So why not simply extend your  logic using "country <> sales org <> address". The real sales proess is any how related to a sales org; As we clearly talk about a parameter symbol: no change (assumption) needed in WWI template. In doing so you have a clear address determintion.

But yes: if you would try to go back now using only SAP standard symbol: you must "regenerated" your released documents.

Most of the companies i have a talk with are not using SAP standard for Chapter 1.

Wish you the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Billing Document Requests number range
entry.content:
Hello ISoma,

As a configuration expert, you can maintain number range intervals by performing the Define Number Ranges for Billing Documents configuration activity. Find this activity by using the search function in your configuration environment. For more information, see Configuration Environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Morę information You can find in this link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/0df51b26f0514d0082b5fb8ff6ff99cb.html

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Mass SPRO Special Procurement Key config options
entry.content:
Hi Jeff008, 

my recommendation is to open SE16N in editable mode. Filter result for specific key for table T460T. Update data (best way is to export to Excel file, update there and copy paste to se16n). Next add this table to transport order to pass data to other environments. 

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Calling Custom Adobe form in ABAP On Cloud (Embedded Steampunk)
entry.content:
Hi,
 
I have a problem with this part of the code. Copy the class from git.
When executing this method:
DATA(ls_template) = lo_store->get_template_by_name( iv_get_binary = abap_false
iv_form_name = 'xxxx' 
iv_template_name = 'yyyy' ).
 
 
After debugging, I verified that in the "get_template_by_name" method, 
it is not assigning the value to the "lr_data" field.
the "lv_json_response" field is filled in
 
 
DATA(lv_json_response) = lo_response->get_text( ).
DATA lr_data TYPE REF TO data.
lr_data = /ui2/cl_json=>generate(
  json = lv_json_response
  pretty_name = /ui2/cl_json=>pretty_mode-camel_case
).
 
 
Any tips?

entry.title: Re: Standard Localization - Organizational Structure
entry.content:
Hi talke,

there is no list for this. Predefined organizational unit are install during installing Best Practices for specific counties.

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: How sorting works in an internal table in given case??
entry.content:
Dear ABAPer_1631,

When you execute this sort command SORT lt_result BY field1 ASCENDING field2 DESCENDING you'll get a follow Table:

Primary key: field1 (ASCENDING)
- All rows have field1 = 12345
- Since every value is identical, the order remains unchanged

Secondary key: field2 (DESCENDING)
- Date fields (TYPE D) are stored internally as an integer in the format YYYYMMDD
- With DESCENDING, the comparison is lexicographic/numeric 'backwards' (newer dates (larger numbers) come before older dates (smaller numbers)).

Stable sort for identical keys
- If two records share the same values in field1 and field2, their original insertion order is preserved.

i) Which record appears first: 01.04.2012 or 04.11.2020?
- Both entries actually share field2 = 20.12.2022.
- The sort criteria are exhausted, so they remain in their original order:
○ Record with field4 = 01.04.2012 (originally row 3)
○ Record with field4 = 04.11.2020 (originally row 4)
-> 01.04.2012 appears before 04.11.2020.


ii) How does the date comparison work?
- Date fields (TYPE D) are stored internally in the format YYYYMMDD.
- Compared lexicographically/numerically For DESCENDING: 20221220 > 20201104 > 20130101 > 20120401

-> Newest dates end up at the top.
I hope my explanation has provided some clarity.

Best regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Re: Change company code from XYZ to ABC
entry.content:
Hi, 

there is no solution to change company code. You need to create all master data manually or using some development for new company code. 

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Cost Distribution
entry.content:
Hi SAP Professionals,

I am currently facing an issue with cost distribution in SAP HCM. Despite accurate maintenance of Infotype 0027 (Cost Distribution), the system appears to disregard this data during the payroll posting run, resulting in no impact on the SAP payroll posting.

HCM (Human Capital Management)

entry.title: Intercompany Financial Posting 1GP - Different tax countries are not permitted
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to post an intercompany financial transaction between two company codes (different countries) and we get this error message:

Different tax countries are not permitted

Taxes are always posted in the initial company code for cross-company-code transactions and stored in the tax information (BSET) of the initial company code. It has not been defined how taxes are subsequently handled when the countries of the company codes involved are different. An integrated company in the sales tax sense can no longer apply because the taxes relate to company codes for different countries.

System Response

Such transactions are not defined and are not permitted.

What is the point then of this test script if this isn't allowed? Why isn't this allowed? The error message is kind of blurry to me. We haven't included any taxes, the tax rate is 0% and the tax code used was V0. It works if the company codes are from the same country but unfortunately that's not useful in our case.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Workflow approval via Integration Center
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe,

In what scenario do you need to use integration center to approve workflow?

there are API queries that you can use to approve workflow though.

Regards,

Reza

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 - SFSL-14795 - Redesigned Continuous Performa...
entry.content:
@BalajiNudurupat  With this new change of RBP the difference is 

Earlier  release we were having a single permission for continuous performance and another one for continuous feedback ,But with this change we can now control if we have to give only permission to view these functionalities or also permission to make changes for these functionalities ,That means in simple words we can control view permission and edit permission with dedicated permission with this release change.

Hope this clear .

Please mark this as Answer so other can get benefit of this.

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: AP Invoices content repository
entry.content:
Hi,

 

i would like to know how can we access the content repository of AP invoices in SAP 2SL public cloud.

entry.title: Achieving Peak Performance of the Financial Close
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the availability of the SAP Cloud ERP Private, financial closing add-on to better help customers achieve ‘peak performance’ of their financial closing process.  The add-on also demonstrates the power and the flexibility of our partner ecosystem because the package includes SAP Account Substantiation and Automation by BlackLine, financial review option.  

This add-on has the following benefits for customers executing a financial close:

  • Run closing activities continuously on granular financial data before period-end for faster automated financial close
  • Ensure data consistency and integration including a ​complete audit trail
  • Eliminate inaccurate and repetitive intercompany closing processes with transactional-level intercompany matching and reconciliation​
  • facilitating unparalleled automation between accounting and corporate close
  • Import financial and non-financial data from multiple sources easily
  • Orchestrate and accelerate closing execution with collaboration workflows and full documentation to ensure compliance
  • Proactively analyze your financial statements with document variance commentary and account sign-off

Please see the Accounting and Financial Close | SAP Financial Management page for more product details and success stories.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay Forecasting Report
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

I am currently looking into the Forecasting Functionalities and have a few questions:

1. Is my understanding correct, that forecasting does not consider already existing timeslices. Eg. Bonus Year is Jan-Dec and I run a forecast in June. One employee is on Bonus Plan 1 with Business Goal A in January, and was moved to receive Bonus Plan 2 with Business Goal B as of February. Does the Forecast Export consider then the uploaded forecasting values for both entities? I tested this case, but the numbers on the export for that user were exactly the same in both cases (1 timeslice and 2 timeslices).

2. In several SAP notes is says that forecasting does not work for Templates with direct payout function type. What exactly is meant by this, because I have such a template, however, the system still provides the forecast results?

3. What is the purpose of the start and end date when setting up the bonus forecast? I set up 2, 1 from 01.Jan-31.Jan and one from 01.Jan-31.Dec. Both provided the exact same numbers.

4. In my template I didnt upload forecast amounts via Business Goals File. However, when I export the Forecasting Report I still see values in the column "Business Forecast 1 Percent". Why is this? Does this have to do with using a direct payout function template?

 

Thank you and best regards,

Rebecca

Rebecca

entry.title: Re: How to navigate from RFKK_MA_SCHEDULER to FQEVENTS
entry.content: Hi, RFKK_MA_SCHEDULER should be debugged like a background job via SM50. You  may also put a sample code with do..enddo loop with conditional exit

entry.title: Re: Creating a sales order with reference to an automatic purchase order via IDoc
entry.content: Good effort, thanks Mert.

entry.title: ME2O/ME2ON does not extracts Reservations for MRP Area
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

As part of an SAP ECC to S/4HANA migration project for a multinational manufacturing company, we are implementing a reorganization of the Consignment Processing by adopting MRP Areas for vendors, replacing the previously used model.

In line with the guidelines provided by SAP Note 2516504 and this blog, we have configured the system to avoid the needing of creating specific material master data for the MRP Area and the automatic generation of transfer postings (movement type 541 O) from the Plant to the MRP Area to cover these requirements due to the MRP RUN (MD02, they don't use the MRP LIVE).

The planning process via MRP and the subsequent creation of transfer postings are functioning correctly. However, we have encountered unexpected behavior when using the ME2O (Stock provided to vendor) and ME2ON (Monitoring of stock provided to vendor) transactions.

Specifically, the ME2O/ME2ON transactions fail to extract the transfer postings if the components involved do not have specific material master data at the MRP Area level (i.e., there is no corresponding record in the MDMA table for the MRP Area in question). Consequently, it is impossible to use these transactions to initiate the shipment of components to the subcontractor.

This observed behavior seems to conflict with SAP guidelines, which would suggest that the creation of material master data at the MRP Area level is not a necessary prerequisite for the correct functioning of the scenario with MRP Areas for Consignment Processing.

Therefore, I would kindly like to request your support in understanding whether we have overlooked any fundamental configuration steps.

I'm available to provide further details on our configuration and the tests performed.

Thanks in advange

entry.title: change PO item short text when transferring shipment cost
entry.content:
shipment cost documents settles freight costs to purchase order. During this transfer, I need to programmatically change the short text of the PO item (EKPO-TXZ01) using ABAP, based on some logic derived from the shipment cost document (e.g., service type, carrier, etc.).

please advice proper badi, user exit, FM that should be used to achieve that.

 

 

ABAP Development #

entry.title: Re: Bem vindos a Comunidade SAP de Londrina!
entry.content:
ótima iniciativa, Bora para cima!

Sou aqui do interior do Paraná, espero poder ajudar nesse movimento.

Já atuo alguns anos no ecossistema SAP.

entry.title: Re: I created a new MM pricing condition that affects PB0...
entry.content:
Hi LuisCaFri,

to separate purchace price variance, or other kind of variances, in their nature you should use specific cost component in your material valuation and have an active actual costing and actual cost component split.

Without these one options is to determine the difference between gl account for material purchase, tariff costs by confronting it with the realeased standard cost estimate for the purchased material. 

Otherwise you could try to avoid debiting the purchased material with the new MM condition and register them in another CO object (i.e. cost center) to be able to allocate them to materials.

These solutions differs greatly and their effectivness rely on how the business model regarding inventory valuation is handled.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hi community! I am glad to join this group. It is great to have a place to like that and connect with others community members!

entry.title: Re: How run the Material ledger for FG & SFG with Price c...
entry.content:
Hi,

it should not possible to set multilevel price determination with price control V, outside specific scenarios such as split valuation.

It is not possible to use to run CKMLCP or AVR on materials that use transaction based price detemination.

And it is not adviced to use moving average for semifinished or finished goods. There are various KBA and sap notes in this regard (https://me.sap.com/notes/81682/E).

entry.title: Re: Valuation WIP in different ledgers w/o Material Ledger/Actual Costing
entry.content:
Hi,

What do you mean by "we need to post the material consumptions on the production order in different ledgers" ?

Without UPA material ledger costs are based on 0L.

But you can post differently the calculated WIP by try using different result analysis key and a updating the configuration in TCODE OKG8 to post in different accounting principles.

entry.content:

Phlavio

entry.title: Wrong password for new company database
entry.content:
Hi SAP B1 Experts,

I facing an issue with a newly created company DB. I'm unable to access the company since the system throws an error message "Incorrect password". Whatever password I enter, the system still reject it. I have checked all SAP services, B1Site user status and password are OK. I'm using SAP B1 v10 FP 2308.

Please can someone could give an idea on how to solve this issue?

Thanks for your support

Muledi

entry.title: Re: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Yes, surprisingly there is no reference for BSP under Work Zone restrictions.
Source: Restrictions | SAP Help Portal

But before refactoring anything I suggest actually to review if such apps still make sense or can be replaced by standard apps. Something created in 2007 could have a standard solution in 2025. Do not only check S/4HANA common knowledge but also some hidden business functions.

If still make sense, you can evaluate if it's SAPUI5 freestyle, Fiori Elements, CAP, a mix of them or anything else based on the tools and services available.

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT

What's New for SAP Business Technology Platform

Thanks,

Sijin

Iza

some knowledge)

entry.title: How to Export a role collection in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Can we export role collections in BTP security?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Export a role collection in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello!

This is Vini from SAP.

There is no direct way to export roles in SAP BTP.
But you can use the REST API as described at ##3492291 - How to Export Role Collections from Subaccount A to Subaccount B?

To facilitate the access to this solution, below are the steps described in the KBA:

Use the REST API for Role Collections in order to:

  1. Retrieve the Role Collection list from Subaccount A
  2. Then Create the same Role Collections in Subaccount B
  3. And finalize with Adding roles to these Role Collections in Subaccount B 

Thank you!
Vini
SAP Support

how this is aligned with clean core

entry.title: Re: eDocument not not relevant"
entry.content: Thank you for your hint. I allready activeted edoc in FIBF. I made all Customzing for Germany. And did the Progammierung for the Badi Edoc_Adaptor merthod is_rrelevant as abap_true too. when I started debugging for edoc_cockpit. It did not go to the Badi too. Any Idea!!!

entry.title: Re: How to identify Login user as Manager in Add new Empl...
entry.content:
Hello @karthik1gobburu ,

To achieve the desired result as per your requirement you can create business rule as attached:

You need to

  • Create one variable for Login User.
  • In the IF part of the business rule, you need to check the value of the Supervisor (Is Manager?) field for the logged-in user. If the Supervisor value is set to "Yes" for the logged-in user, then he/she is considered a manager, and this logic will produce the desired result.

I believe this solution meets your requirement.

 

KR😊

Mrutyunjay Kumar

entry.title: Re: AP Invoices content repository
entry.content:
Hello @Shama321 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2SL), AP invoice attachments can be accessed via Fiori apps like "Manage Supplier Invoices", using the Attachments section. The documents are stored in SAP DMS or via ArchiveLink. Proper roles (e.g., SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT) are required. Use SAP_COM_0109 communication arrangements or connect via BTP-based content services for integration or backend access.

Feel free to let me know if you need any more support.

Thank you and kind regards.

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Join our Live Influencing Session at ASUG Annual Conference in Orlando!
entry.content: Philippe, definitely a good way to hand in an improvement request via the Continuous Influence service. Keeping my fingers crossed for many supporters! 👍

entry.title: Costing sheet not calculating the Overhead amount when the Standard Cost is created CK11N
entry.content:
Costing sheets attached to the costing variant through valuation variant working well in EPT system.

We created the same costing sheets in DEV system and transported them to TST system, we compared each entry of costing sheet between TST and EPT and were looking perfectly correct.

But when we created the cost estimate for the same material and plant both in TST and EPT, TST failed and EPT was successful, as it was calculating the Overheads through costing sheet.

We are not sure if this was due to Transport and we could not see any issues, unless there is a bug in the system while being transported

entry.title: Re: AP Invoices content repository
entry.content: Hi Chuma, thank you for the guidance. My client is already using the attachments in manage supplier invoices, i can work with BASIS support to assign the role you mentioned. However, i would request if you can provide step by step guide on how to access that AP invoice repository on 2SL. I am not very familiar with SAP DMS, archive link, SAP_COM_0109 commmunication arrangements or BTP-based content services.

entry.title: Re: Transport in 2 SL in public cloud
entry.content: Hi Jeremy,

entry.title: Re: Transport in 2 SL in public cloud

entry.content: Dear Peterstoll, what system and environment do you use?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Riga - May 29, 2025
entry.content: Hmmmmmmmm. Noted......

entry.title: Re: Flexible Upload of Reported Financial Data
entry.content: I think the requirement is to have the upload from backend server instead of frontend. Customer may create own program by referring to transaction UJBR to evaluate the code to retrieve the content from the file on the server directory.

entry.title: Re: Multiple business volume selection in condition contract settlement
entry.content: @ParamSharma yes I was able to achieve this requirement using additional business volume tables and selection groups. Also in the settlement pricing procedure a second condition type is required for Total business volume in addition to the business volume. The rebate total condition type must be defined in the profiles for business volume determination as "target field". The additional BV table must be marked as "BV table only for total amount conditions" and assigned to the specified selection group. Also, a scale base pricing routine is required to base the scales on the rebate total condition type. The settings are very tricky.

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT
entry.content: Hi Sijin, thanks for the update. It is helpful!

entry.title: [1H 2025] Compilation of Rich Text Editor Issues and Known Issues (Recruiting Management)
entry.content:
The Rich Text Editor (RTE) is a shared UI component that allows users to enter rich content, including formatted text, links, images, videos, and source code. To ensure a consistent user experience across the suite, we have standardized the SAP Rich Text Editor on Bizx pages, including Recruiting.

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/1h-2025-compilation-of-rich-text-editor-issues-and-known-issues-recruiting/ba-p/14104482#M20096

entry.title: Re: Consuming External API’s in ABAP Code Snippets
entry.content:
Hi @k_sood ,

Instead of storing client secret can we use Oauth profile.

If you have used oauth profile kindly share the logic to get Token using oauth profile .

Thanks

@Sriram961

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
@liyugang You need to have note https://me.sap.com/notes/3450882 applied to your backend.
If you are unable to do this you can to your SAP GUI settings, Preferences -> Web AS ABAP -> Source Editor and uncheck "Enable source editor".

entry.title: Connect to Sybsase DB using isql
entry.content:
Hi all,

I've created a new environment via Cloud Appliance Library.

Template: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP and SAP BW 7.50 SP02 on SAP ASE 16.0

I can SSH to the server, however can't connect to the DB.

 

sid-aba:~ # /sybase/ABA/OCS-16_0/bin/isql -S ABA -U SA -P MyPassword
Msg 4002, Level 14, State 1:
Server 'ABA':
Login failed.
CT-LIBRARY error:
  ct_connect(): protocol specific layer: external error: The attempt to connect to the server failed.

 

I've tried all the users which are mentioned in the giude - https://caldocs.hana.ondemand.com/caldocs/help/6f453362-60ef-4498-9f2b-9cc423f57280_Getting_Started_Guide_v75.pdf 

 

Any ideas what is the root cause?

The password is the same one which was used during the creation of the template.

entry.title: Re: Cost Distribution
entry.content:
Path: Payroll->Payroll: International->Reporting for Posting Payroll Results to Accounting->New General Ledger: Distribution of Liabilities According to Expenses-> Central Settings->Define Type of Distribution

 

Check the above settings

entry.title: Re: Data Attribute Recommendation: 6-Month Support Window for New and Existing Deployments
entry.content: 6 months seems a very short period !! App devs need  to be constantly looking at this now.

entry.title: SAP B1 UI Templates
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

Is it possible to add any new tabs in the starndard (default) report provided by SAP by using "Edit Form UI"?

Our clients in UK used "BAS report generation", They asked me to add the following information to check each information smoother during BAS report generation process.

  • Description of transactions
  • Business Partner

I used UI API, but I could not add new tab.

 

 

 

 

 

Our B1 version is as follows:

  • SAP Business One 10.0  SP 2408

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Connect to Sybsase DB using isql
entry.content: This is not related to Sybase Mainframe Connect, but I have added the tag for Adaptive Server Enterprise (ASE)

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Programming in ABAP - Part 3 - An Example - ABAP RTTS
entry.content:
If all you need is Element Text there is not need of any extra FM or select query

* Calling FM to get data element text
        CALL FUNCTION 'WCGW_DATA_ELEMENT_TEXT_GET'
          EXPORTING
            i_data_element = lw_field_info-rollname
            i_language     = sy-langu
          IMPORTING
            e_scrtext_m    = ls_comp_detail-descr
          EXCEPTIONS
            error          = 1.
        IF ls_comp_detail-descr IS INITIAL.
          ls_comp_detail-descr = wa_components-name.
        ENDIF.

Instead you can get data element text as show below.

" Fetch field label (medium text) if the component is DDIC-based
IF lo_elem_def IS BOUND AND lo_elem_def->is_ddic_type( ) = 'X'.
  " Get the medium-length label from the associated Data Element
  ls_comp_detail-desc = lo_elem_def->get_ddic_field( )-scrtext_m.
ELSE.
  " Fallback: use component name if it's a generic or non-DDIC type
  ls_comp_detail-desc = wa_components-name.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: RSA Encryption in ABAP
entry.content:

Any idea what I am doing incorrect.

entry.title: How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with WalkMe in SAP Landscapes
entry.content:
As enterprises move toward large-scale transformations such as migrating to SAP S/4HANA, rolling out SAP SuccessFactors, or adopting SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) ,one critical challenge often slows momentum: user adoption.

Even the best-designed SAP systems can fall short when users are overwhelmed, untrained, or unsure how to navigate new workflows. That’s where WalkMe, SAP’s Digital Adoption Platform (DAP) partner, becomes a game-changer not just for end users, but for Enterprise Architects (EAs) driving capability-led transformations.

This blog outlines how WalkMe's key features align with Enterprise Architects (EAs) responsibilities, illustrating their strategic relevance in driving architectural governance, user experience, and enterprise-wide transformation success. It maps product capabilities directly to the value Enterprise Architects are expected to deliver from enforcing UX standards and improving data quality, to operationalizing capability models and enabling adaptive experiences. Each section presents not just what WalkMe does, but why it matters to an EA, and how EAs can actively engage to drive adoption, governance, and business transformation outcomes. As SAP landscapes become more modular, user-centric, and data-driven, the need for Enterprise Architects to lead this transformation — with tools like WalkMe as enablers has never been more critical.

 

1. Smart Walk-Thrus – Turning Process Maps into Guided Journeys

What It Does:
Smart Walk-Thrus guide users step-by-step through complex workflows. They can automate clicks, form inputs, and screen transitions, allowing users to complete tasks without confusion or error.
Learn More

EA Scenario: 

An EA working on a global SAP S/4HANA Transformation with business leads to convert approved process maps into Smart Walk-Thrus. These ensure consistent process execution across business units, reinforcing the target state design defined in EA blueprints.

To move beyond feature descriptions, the table below highlights how specific WalkMe product capabilities translate into enterprise architecture outcomes. This structured view helps Enterprise Architects contextualize each function within their architecture practice — from supporting process standardization to driving onboarding scalability and bridging design-execution gaps.

This format guides EAs in articulating how WalkMe supports capability realization, modular design, and transformation governance across SAP landscapes.

EA Value:

  • Operationalizes standardized business capabilities

  • Reduces deviation from enterprise process models

  • Bridges the design-execution gap by embedding compliance paths

  • Accelerates onboarding and transformation realization

2. Smart Tips – Enforcing UX Standards Through Real-Time Validation

What It Does:
Smart Tips are contextual tooltips helping users enter accurate information and avoid errors without disrupting their workflow. Learn More 

EA Scenario: 

To improve data quality in material master creation, an EA defines Smart Tips aligned with the enterprise data model. These help users enter accurate values and reduce post-entry data cleansing costs.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on Smart Tips

EA Value:

  • Reinforces data governance policies at the point of entry

  • Reduces data inconsistencies and integration rework

  • Supports UX standardization across SAP modules

  • Drives compliance with EA-defined data architecture standards

 

3. ShoutOut – Communicating Change at Scale

 What It Does:
ShoutOuts are in-app banners or pop-ups used to communicate key announcements, training opportunities, or system changes.Learn More 

EA Scenario:

During a phased rollout of SuccessFactors,S/4 or BTP  EAs can map ShoutOuts to align system changes with transformation timelines, reinforcing key messaging from the architecture change plan.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on

EA Value:

  • Enhances enterprise-wide communication strategy

  • Aligns operational communications with architectural transition states

  • Drives awareness of capability rollouts and lifecycle phases

 

4. Launchers – Enabling On-Demand User Support

What It Does:
Launchers are clickable icons that trigger Smart Walk-Thrus, open support documents, or link to external tools, embedded directly into SAP screens. Learn More 

EA Scenario:

An EA defines reusable support assets accessible via Launchers in SAP Fiori, ensuring users can navigate between modular apps while maintaining process continuity.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on Launchers

EA Value:

  • Promotes component reuse and modular process design

  • Reduces pressure on centralized support by enabling embedded guidance

  • Aligns with composable enterprise architecture principles

 

5. WalkMe Insights, Funnels, and Heatmaps – Making Adoption Measurable

What It Does:
WalkMe provides real-time analytics showing how users interact with SAP systems—where they drop off, what they complete, and how effective your guidance is. Learn More 

EA Scenario:

EAs track user behavior during invoice processing to identify drop-off points. Insights reveal misalignment between business capability design and actual execution, prompting iterative improvements.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on WalkMe Insights

EA Value:

  • Introduces continuous feedback into EA governance

  • Informs capability maturity assessments with usage data

  • Supports data-driven prioritization of architecture improvements

6. Global Variables & Conditional Logic – Powering Context-Aware Architecture

What It Does:
WalkMe enables the use of variables, conditional logic, and branching flows to personalize the user journey and dynamically adjust to user roles or previous interactions. Learn More 

EA Scenario:

During a global procurement rollout, EAs configure logic to adjust workflows based on user region, ensuring local compliance within a centralized architecture.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on Global Variables & Conditional Logic

EA Value:

  • Delivers adaptive user journeys tied to EA persona models

  • Supports rules-based personalization without backend changes

  • Reduces cross-functional friction and improves architecture agility

 

7. Menu Organizer – Structuring Content Around Business Capabilities

What It Does:
The Menu Organizer lets you group, categorize, and assign WalkMe content to folders visible to users based on their role, department, or geography. Learn More 

EA Scenario:

EAs collaborating with HR and Finance teams structure WalkMe menus around value streams (e.g., Hire-to-Retire, Procure-to-Pay) for greater role-based relevance.

The following table continues the mapping of WalkMe capabilities to Enterprise Architecture value, with a focus on Menu Organizer

EA Value:

  • Enables federated content ownership aligned with capability domains

  • Reinforces EA-driven role-based access controls 

  • Supports modular support content strategy across large SAP estates

Final Thoughts

WalkMe is no longer just a productivity layer. For Enterprise Architects in SAP-driven organizations, it is a strategic extension of architecture governance, design, and execution.

By embedding WalkMe across your SAP landscape, you can:

  • Translate abstract capability models into real user guidance

  • Deliver personalized, rule-based user experiences without custom development

  • Capture adoption analytics to validate architecture success

  • Build a feedback-driven, composable architecture that evolves with your users

If you're designing, governing, or executing digital transformation with SAP, WalkMe is your blueprint's missing link. Feel free to reach out to the Global Enterprise Architecture Advisory at SAP for collaboration or further insights.

 

 

Thank you 

Ramy Salem ,Global Enterprise Architect at SAP and PhD researcher in Enterprise Architecture AI Agents

TOGAF SAP Certified.

entry.content:
After the upgrade to SAP S/4HANA, we ran MRP.
When MRP was run, automatic schedule lines were updated.
However, before the system update, when MRP was run, purchase requisitions (PRs) were generated.
We do not maintain source lists in the system.
Please provide a solution on how to generate PRs with respect to the MRP run instead of automatic schedule lines

entry.title: Re: Costing sheet not calculating the Overhead amount whe...
entry.content:
Hi,
Have you checked if overheads are maintained in your target system?
As for my understanding in SAP older versions to transport overhead records you had to execute RKAZUTR1 to apply changes to overhead price conditions.

If that is not the issue you should probably double check customizing and master data differences between the systems.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA and Python Integration

Hi @mitalk ,

Yes, It is allowed to perform CRUD (Create, Read, Update & Delete) operations using python scripts from outside S/4.

You can explore the following blogs for the same - 

Python hana_ml: define HANA table and upload(creat... - SAP Community

Connecting HANA DB using python Language - SAP Community

Regards,
Manisha Chaturvedi

entry.title: Re: How to implement "readonly : update" for draft instances
entry.content:
Hi,

I am writing a RAP service with V4 version. I am using the instance control to control the enablement of the operation in my page. For custom action and fields, it works fine, but for the standard operation "Edit" it ignores my code (I of course put update (features: instance) in the BDEF). The "Edit" button stays active no meter what value were assigned to it in the feature control method. When I worked with V2 the same code  worked just fine.

Another thing that isn't working on the V4 version is the annotation sortBy, again it worked well for the V2 version, but when I look on the HTTP call I don't even see the order by addition.

@Andre_Fischer

entry.title: Re: How to set email notification for rework of Sales document for Flexible workflow
entry.content: Hi, do you have access to the workflow scenario definition? Transaction SWDD_SCENARIO

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 Binded Text inside a ScrollContainer
entry.content:
Hi @Silvio_Stoykov,

So, the main issue arises due to how the content is loaded.
When the <Text> control contains hardcoded text, the scroll height is fully available during rendering.
However, when the text is bound to a model (e.g., {/long/text}), it loads asynchronously. As a result, the scrollable height isn’t immediately calculated, causing your scroll logic to assume there's no need to scroll — and enabling the button too early.

Assuming you Button is like the following - 

<Button id="acceptButton" text="Accept" enabled="false" press="onAccept" />

 Do not bind button enabled to any model property.
 Only set it to true in the scroll listener when the bottom is reached.

And to answer your second question -> Instead of setTimeOut you can use "addEventDeleagate". It attaches your handler to a control’s lifecycle event, so your function runs immediately after that event happens. This is more reliable than waiting a fixed delay with "setTimeout", which guesses when the UI is ready but can be wrong.

this.getView().addEventDelegate({
  onAfterShow: () => {
    this.handleScrollbar();
  }
});

This ensures handleScrollbar() runs right after the view is fully shown, without arbitrary delays.
Reference - https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/api/sap.ui.core.Element%23methods/Summary

Hope this helps 🙂

Regards,
Manisha Chaturvedi

entry.title: Re: New in Production Accounting – Event-Based Production Cost Posting
entry.content:

Anxiously waiting for the proper answer/solution on that

entry.title: ATO for Sales Quotation

entry.title: ⚡Unlocking the Power of LLMs in CAP: Understanding llms.txt and llms-full.txt
entry.content:
As AI continues to reshape how we build and interact with software, the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) is embracing this shift by introducing two new files: llms.txt and llms-full.txt. These files are designed to enhance the way large language models (LLMs) like SAP Build Code or ChatGPT or any AI IDE understand and interact with CAP documentation and projects.

In this blogpost, we’ll explore what these files are, how they benefit users, how to integrate them into your workflows, and what limitations you should be aware of.

LLMs.txt is a new web standard designed to help reasoning engines understand website content better. It addresses the limitations of AI systems by providing a format optimized for AI processing, removing non-essential information, and presenting content more efficiently. The concept was popularized by Mintlify, which added support for LLMs.txt to their docs platform. The file format includes two types: /llms.txt for navigation and structure, and /llms-full.txt for comprehensive documentation. Several tools are available to help generate these files, and their adoption is expected to grow as AI-readability becomes crucial for web content.

 

What exactly is an LLMs.txt file?

LLMs.txt is a markdown file with a specific structure. The specification defines two distinct files:

  1. /llms.txt: A streamlined view of your documentation navigation to help AI systems quickly understand your site’s structure
  2. /llms-full.txt: A comprehensive file containing all your documentation in one place

Benefits for Users

Improved AI Assistance

By referencing llms.txt or llms-full.txt in prompts, users can guide LLMs to more relevant and accurate answers. This is especially useful when working with AI tools that support prompt augmentation or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Faster Onboarding

New developers can use AI tools enhanced with these files to get up to speed quickly, asking natural language questions and receiving answers grounded in official CAP documentation.

Context-Aware Development

When integrated into development environments or chat-based assistants, these files help LLMs provide suggestions, code snippets, and explanations that are aligned with CAP’s best practices.

How to Integrate and Use

  1. Prompt Augmentation: When using an LLM, include a reference to https://cap.cloud.sap/llms.txt in your prompt. For example:

    “Using the documentation at https://cap.cloud.sap/llms.txt, explain how to define a service in CAP.”

  2. RAG Pipelines: If you're building your own AI assistant or chatbot, you can ingest the URLs from llms-full.txt into your vector database or document retriever to enable semantic search over CAP docs.

  3. IDE Integration: Advanced setups can use these files to power AI-based code completion or documentation lookup tools within IDEs like VS Code.

Limitations and Considerations

While llms.txt and llms-full.txt are powerful tools, they come with a few caveats:

Static Nature

These files are snapshots of documentation URLs. They don’t automatically reflect real-time changes unless regularly updated.

No Content, Just Links

They don’t contain the actual documentation content—only links. LLMs still need access to the linked pages to provide meaningful answers.

Access Control

If your AI tool doesn’t have access to the CAP documentation (e.g., due to network restrictions), referencing these files won’t help.

LLM Limitations

Even with these files, LLMs may still hallucinate or misinterpret complex CAP concepts if not properly grounded with the actual content.

Final Thoughts

The introduction of llms.txt and llms-full.txt is a forward-thinking move by the CAP team, making it easier for developers to harness the power of AI in their workflows. Whether you're building intelligent assistants, enhancing developer productivity, or simply exploring CAP, these files offer a structured way to bring AI and documentation closer together.

 

Reference Article of /llms

https://llmstxt.org/

entry.title: ATP & Production Capacity
entry.content: How standard ATP consider Production Capacity against requested qty to provide accurate Delivery?

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap May '25
entry.content:
The sun didn't make an appearance at our latest Sydney Stammtisch....but plenty of Sydneysiders did.  We had a good mix of regulars and new faces.

I got some good feedback on the venue (the Keg & Brew).  There was plenty of room up on the rooftop, the pizzas hit the spot and even though it did rain a bit we stayed cosy and dry under the awning.  We will definitely be back, and this time we won't be waiting 8 years!

I'm currently sounding people out about August 14th for the next event. Let me know in the comments what you think, and whether you have any good venues to suggest.

Thanks to @jeff_t for some of the photos.  As always I'm sorry not to have got everyone in a photo

entry.title: Re: eDocument not not relevant"
entry.content: The Reason was I took the edoc tyoe DE_INV for SD-Invoice because I wanted to create edoc from SD-Invoice. I added now the edoc type DE-CII (Germany Cross Industry Invoice) and it works now. That is funny.
